Fig. 3. Agency (npcsh score) versus intelligence (MMLU). The regression line (r ≈ 0.8) with 1σ band; outliers beyond 1σ are labeled.
TABLE I Benchmark results across model families.
Fig. 2. Benchmark score versus parameter count across model families.
Fig. 6. Pass rate by model and task category. Models ordered by overall score (left to right); categories by mean difficulty (top to bottom).

Fig. 1. The CAT data layer. Left: context files (orange) scope teams of NPCs (blue). Right: Jinxes (green) compose as a DAG rooted at python. Level-1 Jinxes (chat, sh, web search, screenshot) each use python as their engine. Level-2 Jinxes combine level1 steps: react chains chat + python, computer use chains chat + screenshot + sh, and delegate uses chat + sh. Shared parents (e.g. chat used by all three composites) make the graph a DAG rather than a tree. Dashed arrows show tool-catalog assignment to NPCs.
